name: figma-full-extraction
prompt: Extract the full design system from the currently open Figma file
assertions: Produces a tokens.md with at least one color collection and one spacing collection
Each token has values for all available modes
Color values are valid hex or rgba format
Spacing values are multiples of 4 (or the detected grid unit)
Produces a component-states.md with at least 3 component types
Each component has Default and at least one interactive state
No placeholder or TODO markers in final output
Output files match the make-UI reference format structure
Extraction metadata (source, date, pipeline) present at top of each file

name: incremental-page-extraction
prompt: Extract component patterns from a new SnowUI page and merge into existing references
assertions: Existing reference data is preserved unchanged
New components are added with correct state matrix format
New behavioral rules are appended with source attribution
Conflicts are flagged with [CONFLICT] markers, not silently overwritten
Token references in new components resolve to existing token names
Post-merge diff shows only additions, no deletions of existing content

name: screenshot-extraction
prompt: Extract design patterns from a screenshot of a dashboard UI
assertions: Identifies layout pattern (column count, sidebar presence)
Extracts approximate color palette (at least 3 colors)
Identifies component types present (card, table, button, etc.)
Uncertain values marked with [approximate]
Output is a valid reference file, not raw notes
If existing token table loaded, maps colors to nearest tokens

name: non-snowui-extraction
prompt: Extract design tokens from a non-SnowUI Figma file (e.g., Material Design, Ant Design)
assertions: Produces tokens.md even when token names differ from SnowUI conventions
Maps foreign token naming to semantic categories (background, text, border)
Does not assume SnowUI-specific patterns (opacity hierarchy, 4-mode system)
Component states extracted even with different state naming conventions
Output schemas are generic, not SnowUI-hardcoded
